title: Lithocarpus dealbatus
intro: Lithocarpus dealbatus, also known as the Tibetan beech, is a deciduous tree species native to the mountainous regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus dealbatus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lithocarpus dealbatus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory infections, fever, and other ailments.
taxonomy: Lithocarpus dealbatus is a species of tree in the beech family, Fagaceae. This species is native to Asia. It belongs to the genus Lithocarpus, which is a group of deciduous and evergreen trees. Lithocarpus dealbatus is further classified into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Tracheophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Fagales, family Fagaceae, genus Lithocarpus, and species L. dealbatus. As a member of the Fagaceae family, Lithocarpus dealbatus is closely related to other species of beech and oak trees.
